behaviors.description: Required fields are validated before downstream processing; missing fields return one error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (validator must gate insurance/triage/scheduling), not just field-check logic. REAL-INFRA/LLM/MULTI-STEP: none required. Candidate Integration test: setup intake request missing DOB+insurance ID with downstream services stubbed; assert error lists both fields and no downstream calls happened; catches bug where pipeline continues despite validation errors. Candidate Unit test: setup validator-only call; assert missing-field list; catches formatting bug but misses accidental downstream invocation. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text has no real OS/network requirement; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no model-output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the intake orchestrator with missing required fields and spies/stubs for downstream modules; verify aggregated missing-field error and zero downstream invocations.

behaviors.description: DOB validation rejects future dates, >150-year-old dates, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is pure validation logic at one function boundary (MOCK-MISS says Unit floor). No infra, no model, no pipeline coupling needed. Candidate Unit test: setup table of invalid DOB strings/dates; assert validator returns correct error codes/messages; catches boundary and parsing defects. Candidate Integration test would also pass but adds no unique failure detection for this behavior. Rejection gate: higher levels disqualified because behavior text is exclusively local parsing/range rules.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit test for DOB validator covering future date, 150+ years old cutoff, and malformed string inputs.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility call sends correct patient ID/plan code and maps eligible/not eligible/pending correctly.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior targets request/response contract with external payer API; real HTTP serialization/status handling can fail while mocks pass (MOCK-HIDE). Candidate System test: setup local HTTP test server emulating payer responses and inspect received payload; assert outbound patient ID+plan code and status mapping for 3 responses; catches wire-format or transport-layer mapping bugs. Candidate Integration test with mocked client asserts method args but may miss actual HTTP payload/encoding mismatches. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow disqualified by behavior text (deterministic API states, no AI decision chain).
behaviors.test_description: Exercise insurance client against a real HTTP test endpoint that validates payload fields and returns each eligibility state; verify mapped intake status.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, intake marks eligibility pending and continues instead of blocking.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: unreachable condition is a network/transport failure mode; mocked success/error objects can hide real timeout/connection exceptions (MOCK-HIDE). Candidate System test: setup payer URL to non-routable/test server down; assert eligibility becomes pending and orchestration continues to triage/scheduling; catches unhandled socket/timeout exceptions. Candidate Integration test: mock client throws UnreachableError; asserts fallback, but can miss real exception classes/timeout paths from HTTP stack. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow rejected because no AI-output correctness requirement in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake with real HTTP failure conditions for payer call and verify fallback-to-pending plus continued downstream pipeline execution.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation payload includes reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module wiring (payload builder + triage client), not model judgment. LLM-DEP disqualified: behavior checks request composition only. Candidate Integration test: setup configured rubric + symptoms and stub model endpoint recording request body; assert both appear in payload; catches dropped-config or field-mapping bugs. Candidate Unit test on serializer alone may miss orchestrator forgetting to pass rubric. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected by behavior text focusing on invocation payload, not output quality; System rejected because in-process stub can expose this failure.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke triage service end-to-end with stubbed model client and assert outgoing payload contains symptoms and rubric fields.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage assigns high urgency for emergency symptom combinations (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model medical reasoning output; deterministic stubs always hide this failure. Candidate Agentic test: submit emergency symptom cases to live triage model repeatedly/with threshold policy; assert urgency is high; catches model/prompt regression in clinical urgency classification. Candidate Integration test with stubbed 'high' response only verifies plumbing and cannot detect misclassification. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because behavior is a single model decision, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run live triage calls on emergency symptom fixtures and assert returned urgency category is high under production prompt/model settings.

behaviors.description: Triage result parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field, not only a numeric score.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is parser-to-storage handoff (field persistence), not just parsing function. No required real OS/network/model quality. Candidate Integration test: setup triage response fixture with score+explanation, persist through parser/repo layer, assert DB record contains explanation text column; catches dropped-field mapping bug. Candidate Unit test on parser output misses repository schema/write omission. Rejection gate: System rejected since local in-process DB/module wiring is sufficient; Agentic rejected because explanation content quality is not evaluated here.
behaviors.test_description: Feed deterministic triage response through parser plus persistence layer and verify stored record includes non-empty explanation field.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage explanation cites case-specific symptoms and urgency rationale, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: this is output-quality behavior (specificity/rationale), impossible to validate with fixed mocks. Candidate Agentic test: send varied symptom cases to live model; assert explanation references case symptoms and urgency reasoning rubric (not generic template); catches prompt/model drift to boilerplate output. Candidate Integration test with canned response always passes and cannot detect real quality regression. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because requirement concerns one model output step.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate live triage explanations for symptom-specific rationale using real model responses across representative cases.

behaviors.description: Scheduling picks first available slot matching urgency SLA (high same-day, medium within 48h).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when scheduling logic interacts with calendar data ordering/availability windows. No mandatory real OS/network/model. Candidate Integration test: setup calendar DB with mixed occupied/free slots across today/next 48h; assert chosen slot is earliest meeting urgency SLA; catches query/order or SLA-window bugs. Candidate Unit test of pure selector can miss repository query filtering/timezone handoff issues. Rejection gate: System rejected because local DB wiring can reproduce behavior; Agentic/Workflow rejected (no AI dependence).
behaviors.test_description: Run scheduler against seeded calendar data and verify selected appointment is earliest slot satisfying urgency-specific time constraints.

behaviors.description: Scheduling does not double-book when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is concurrency/transaction handling across scheduler + shared calendar DB modules. Can be reproduced with local DB and concurrent workers; no external network/model required. Candidate Integration test: setup one free slot, run two intake scheduling attempts concurrently, assert only one booking succeeds and second gets next slot/error; catches race/locking defects. Candidate System test adds OS/process realism but not strictly required for this behavior boundary. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text centers shared calendar DB consistency, reproducible in-process with real DB transactions; Agentic/Workflow disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Execute concurrent scheduling operations against shared calendar DB and assert unique slot assignment under race conditions.

behaviors.description: Confirmation includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and visit-type-specific prep instructions.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans data handoff from scheduling/patient context into confirmation renderer; unit template test may miss missing upstream fields. No real infra or model dependency needed. Candidate Integration test: setup completed intake record for visit type, generate confirmation, assert all required fields and correct prep instructions included; catches mapping omissions between modules. Candidate Unit test on message template alone cannot catch orchestrator passing incomplete data. Rejection gate: System/Agentic/Workflow rejected by behavior text (deterministic message composition).
behaviors.test_description: Generate confirmation from integrated intake/scheduling outputs and verify required fields plus visit-type preparation instructions are present.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration (validate→insurance→triage→schedule→confirm) completes correctly with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly scopes to deterministic stubbed triage, so LLM-output correctness is out of scope. This is multi-module orchestration/state handoff, ideal Integration. Candidate Integration test: setup stubs for payer+triage and local DB, run full pipeline, assert stage order, propagated statuses, and final confirmation produced; catches orchestration regressions. Candidate System test with real services is heavier but unnecessary for stated failure mode. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected by explicit text 'deterministic stubbed triage output'; Workflow rejected because no real agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end intake orchestration with deterministic stubs and verify correct stage transitions, persisted state, and final confirmation artifact.

behaviors.description: Full live intake pipeline completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Includes live triage model call inside full pipeline; LLM-DEP applies because real model response can alter downstream path/success. Candidate Agentic test: submit real patient-like payload through production-like stack (live payer + live triage), assert pipeline reaches confirmed appointment automatically; catches failures dependent on real model output and integration. Adjacent Integration test with stub triage may pass while live model output format/content breaks downstream parsing/routing. Rejection gate: Workflow considered but rejected because behavior text includes one agentic decision point (triage), not multiple sequential agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the live end-to-end intake flow using real external dependencies and verify unattended completion from submission to confirmation.
